description_long: This bungalow features a prominent, steeply pitched gable roof with layered eaves and a symmetrical facade. The exterior is constructed from light-colored masonry or brick, accented by darker trim and a series of rectangular windows. The front entrance is set back beneath a covered porch, flanked by columns and accessed via a wide staircase. The surrounding property is enclosed by a low fence and includes paved walkways, small garden plots, and decorative planters. The structure appears to have two main stories, with the upper level partially integrated into the roofline. The overall design is orderly and balanced, with a focus on geometric forms and a welcoming entryway.
description: A symmetrical, two-story bungalow with a steep gable roof, light masonry walls, and a covered front porch, surrounded by a fenced yard and garden plots.
description_view_0: The rear view shows the back facade with a series of evenly spaced windows and a large, uninterrupted roof surface.
description_view_1: The front view highlights the main entrance, covered porch, central staircase, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_2: The opposite rear view mirrors the first, showing the back of the bungalow and the garden plots along the sides.
description_view_3: The opposite front view again emphasizes the entryway, porch, and the balanced design of the facade.
